author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

The feature never worked. `Storage::resolve` (now at
`crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:2618`) had a 7-char `IssueId::parse`
fast path and a slug fallback â€” no prefix-match step in between.
Every hex-shaped sub-7-char handle (`9566`, `9566f5`, etc.) fell
straight to the slug resolver and surfaced as `slug_not_found`.
CLAUDE.md's "any unambiguous prefix" claim has been aspirational
since v3 landed.

# Recommendation

Verdict 1 (restore the feature) â€” shipped.

`Storage::resolve` now treats a 1â€“6-char lowercase-hex handle as
an id prefix. Exactly-one match â†’ resolves. Zero matches â†’ new
`Error::IdNotFound` (CLI `id_not_found`). Two-or-more matches â†’
new `Error::AmbiguousPrefix { handle, matches }` (CLI
`ambiguous_prefix`, `details.matches` carries the sorted candidate
list). Non-hex handles still route to the slug resolver and
surface `slug_not_found` as before. Closed issues participate in
the prefix match (same scope as the slug resolver).

The subordinate issue is also resolved: hex-shaped misses no
longer mis-label as `slug_not_found`. New error-kind table rows
in `docs/cli-json.md`; v2.5 â†’ v2.6 changelog entry added.
CLAUDE.md's "Issue-id length" wording tightened to mention the
ambiguity branch.

Manual repro on the live planner now works:

- `jjf show 9566f52` â€” full id, unchanged.
- `jjf show 9566` â€” resolves to the roadmap.
- `jjf show 9` â€” `ambiguous_prefix` listing `9566f52`, `95fb2d6`,
  `99f01ed` (the three open ids starting with `9`).

Changed files:
- `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs` (Error enum + resolve + helper)
- `crates/jjf/src/main.rs` (CliError variants + envelope wiring)
- `crates/jjf-storage/tests/integration.rs` (+6 tests)
- `crates/jjf/tests/type_and_slug.rs` (+4 CLI envelope tests)
- `docs/cli-json.md` (changelog + error-kind table)
- `CLAUDE.md` (Issue-id length section)

`cargo nextest run --workspace`: 469 passing (was 459).

# Confidence

High. The behavior is pinned by 10 new tests covering happy path,
ambiguous, unknown-hex, non-hex-still-routes-to-slug, full-id
regression, and the closed-issue scope. CLI envelope shapes pinned
by tests too â€” scripts pattern-matching on the new kinds have a
contract.

# Open follow-ups

- Scripts pattern-matching on `slug_not_found` for hex-shaped input
  now need to match `id_not_found` too (called out in the v2.6
  changelog).
- If the issue population ever crosses a few thousand, the O(N)
  prefix scan over `snapshot.issues.keys()` could be replaced
  with a sorted-id binary search â€” but that's a non-issue at
  jjforge's current scale.
- No new ticket needed; this closes 4940d78.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Reverted

The prefix-matching implementation landed in `8179f758` was
reverted after user pushback: short handles via slug (already
supported) cover the ergonomic case, and prefix matching adds
ambiguity surface (`ambiguous_prefix` errors, accidental
collisions as the issue count grows) for little additional
benefit.

## Recommendation

Close as **won't-fix** with the docs corrected.
CLAUDE.md's "Issue-id length" section now states explicitly:
pass the full 7-char id or a slug. A 7-char hex handle that
doesn't match any issue surfaces as `issue_not_found` (the
already-existing storage-read error), not via the resolver.

Net delta from this ticket:
- CLAUDE.md docs corrected (the original "often 4 chars is
  enough" claim was wrong).
- `docs/cli-json.md`: no error-kind additions; v2.6 changelog
  removed.
- Resolver, error variants, tests: back to pre-`8179f758` state.

## Confidence

HIGH. All 462 workspace tests pass; manual repro on the live
planner confirms full-id and slug lookups work; prefix
returns `slug_not_found`; nonexistent full id returns
`issue_not_found`.

## Open follow-ups

None.
